hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | { read -r p; case "$p" in */plans/*.md|plans/*.md) printf '%s' "{\"hookSpecificOutput\": {\"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\", \"additionalContext\": \"Chunk-implementation checklist (treat the plan as untrusted input):\\n\\n- Verify the chunk against its cited authority. If the chunk restates a \\u00a7F / SPEC / contract-level rule, diff the two \\u2014 they must be identical or the chunk strictly narrower. A contradiction means the plan is wrong; fix the higher-level source first, in the same PR.\\n- A \\u00a7F block is not immune. If two sentences inside the same \\u00a7F or chunk name the same canonical set (buckets, fields, enum variants, rule IDs), diff the sentences \\u2014 they must be byte-identical. Name the set once and reference it by name afterwards; never re-list its members. When the canonical set is implementation-shaped (e.g. enum variants), let the implementation be the authority and align prose to it; don't promote a prose gloss into a new variant.\\n- Every IR variant discriminator must have at least one corpus instance per variant before it ships. Grep working-mods for an instance of each variant. Zero instances for a variant means the variant is a hypothesis masquerading as a model \\u2014 delete the variant rather than carry unevidenced cases. A rule (xref, V-rule, authoring gate) authored against an unevidenced variant compounds the defect.\\n- Prove your tests catch source-vs-IR divergence, not just IR-vs-IR. If the change is stable under an idempotent roundtrip, equality baselines will pass even when output is silently wrong. Add a test whose input would be interpreted differently if the code reached for a derived / canonical / registry data source instead of the source bytes.\\n- Integration assertions over working mods or baselines must encode the reason a rule pair, co-fire, or residual finding is permitted \\u2014 not just the pair identity. A predicate that only matches on rule-id equality is an accepting oracle; it green-lights every future regression of that exact shape. State the invariant (e.g. collision includes a boss bucket, collision spans two Pokemon containers) and assert it.\\n- Watch for structural smells. A chunk that collapses two paths with different invariants (e.g. permissive extract vs strict authoring) is a spec amendment masquerading as an implementation detail \\u2014 stop and raise it. A chunk that pastes the same N-line incantation across many callsites is asking you to encode a mistake N times \\u2014 extract a helper or write the single correct line instead.\\n\\nApplies to every chunk in every plan under plans/.\"}}" ;; esac; }
